# Atlas (`@steel-dev/atlas`)

> Deep research as a CLI and a TypeScript library. Ask a messy question; Atlas
> scopes it into search angles, fetches pages through Steel Browser, extracts
> verbatim-quoted claims, chases the gaps, adversarially verifies every claim
> with independent voters, and synthesizes a cited Markdown report from the
> claims that survive. Runs on the Vercel AI SDK, so any model provider works.

The value is the verification pipeline: every claim in the report is backed by a
verbatim quote from a fetched source and has survived adversarial voting.
Sources, claims, citations, and structured output are all auditable.

This file is the agent-facing brief — a compact reference for coding agents
building on Atlas. Print it anytime with `atlas docs`. Full prose lives in
README.md.

## Install

```bash
npm install @steel-dev/atlas ai @ai-sdk/anthropic   # + the provider you use
```

Keys (env): a model provider key (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` or `OPENAI_API_KEY`) plus
`STEEL_API_KEY`.

## CLI

```bash
npx @steel-dev/atlas "<question>" > report.md
atlas "<question>" --out report.md --token-limit 5000000
atlas serve            # local web UI
atlas docs             # print this brief
```

Run `atlas --help` for the full flag list.

## SDK — core API

```ts
import { Atlas } from "@steel-dev/atlas";
import { anthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";

const atlas = new Atlas({ model: anthropic("claude-sonnet-4-6") });
const result = await atlas.research("What's changing in browser automation?");
result.markdown;            // cited Markdown report
result.citedSources;        // sources actually cited
result.claims.confirmed;    // verified claims, with quotes + votes
result.stats;               // angles, sources, claims extracted/verified
```

`new Atlas(config)` fields:

- `model` (required) — an AI SDK model instance.
- `leafModel?` — cheaper model for high-volume leaf calls (claim extraction,
  verifier voters).
- `instructions?` — domain framing for the research lead. The main domain knob.
- `tools?` — `Record<string, ResearchTool>` of domain tools (see below).
- `browser?` — `steel(...)`; defaults to Steel from env.
- `search?` — `exa()` / `brave()` to bypass SERP scraping; omit to scrape.
- `defaults?` — default `RunOptions` applied to every run.

Calls:

- `atlas.research(query, opts?)` → `ResearchResult`.
- `atlas.research({ query, outputSchema })` → `StructuredResearchResult`: adds
  `result.data` (typed, filled from verified claims) and `result.basis` (each
  field path → citations + reasoning). Unsupported fields are flagged, not
  invented.
- `atlas.stream(...)` → a handle: `run.fullStream` (async iterable),
  `run.on(type, listener)` (typed events: `fetching`, `claim_verified`,
  `report_delta`, …), `run.result` (resolves at the end).

## Custom tools — the extension point for domain researchers

A tool's results become citable sources: anything passed to `ctx.addSource` is
extracted into claims, adversarially verified, and cited exactly like a fetched
web page.

```ts
import { Atlas, researchTool } from "@steel-dev/atlas";
import { anthropic } from "@ai-sdk/anthropic";
import { z } from "zod";

const atlas = new Atlas({
  model: anthropic("claude-sonnet-4-6"),
  instructions: "You are a clinical evidence analyst. Prefer peer-reviewed sources.",
  tools: {
    pubmedSearch: researchTool({
      description: "Search PubMed. Each result becomes a citable source.",
      inputSchema: z.object({ query: z.string(), limit: z.number().default(5) }),
      execute: async ({ query, limit }, ctx) => {
        const studies = await pubmed.search(query, { limit, signal: ctx.signal });
        for (const s of studies)
          ctx.addSource({ url: s.url, title: s.title, content: s.abstract });
        return studies.map((s) => `- ${s.title} — ${s.url}`).join("\n");
      },
    }),
  },
});
```

`ToolContext`: `addSource({ url, title?, content })`, `log(message)`, `signal?`.

`tools` is a record, so the key is the model-facing tool name — pass the same
tool under different keys to expose different configurations.

## Building a domain researcher

A domain researcher is a small module: `new Atlas({ model, instructions, tools })`,
optionally with an `outputSchema` per run. Compose domain `researchTool`s and let
the verification pipeline do the rest. The `atlas-build-researcher` skill scaffolds
one interactively.

## Notes for agents

- Don't bypass or reimplement the verification pipeline — it is the product.
  Tools only *add sources*; verification stays in the engine.
- Contract types (`ResearchResult`, `ResearchClaim`, `CitedSource`,
  `SourceDocument`, …) are exported from the package. Import them; never copy or
  fork them.
- Any model the AI SDK supports works; install its provider package and pass the
  model instance to `model` (and optionally `leafModel`).
